nodes.summary: A2 single-decision shim for the G4A voice-pick pause point (woken at Arc 2): loads an OperatorVerdict JSON file and resumes the paused production trial.

nodes.name: shim_main
nodes.summary: Validates the verdict file into an OperatorVerdict and drives resume_production_trial for the G4A pause, with fail-loud error reporting.

nodes.name: marcus_interlocutor.py
nodes.summary: The LLM-driven stop-and-chat interlocution REPL (S5): at each paused gate Marcus narrates the real decision card in persona, answers free-form operator turns via the injected chat model, and only after a deterministic guard validates the verb against the gate's allowed set does it confirm and drive resume_production_trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Chat model and I/O are dependency-injected seams: offline tests inject a fake BaseChatModel and scripted input, and any live model call in the offline path raises rather than silently falling through.

nodes.name: StructuredAction
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for the LLM's proposed gate action (verb plus payload) with a parse method that extracts the structured action from a model turn.

nodes.name: ValidatedDecision
nodes.summary: A guard-approved gate decision carrying the validated verb and payload plus a confirm_echo rendering shown to the operator before the engine is driven.

nodes.name: MarcusInterlocutor
nodes.summary: The REPL engine: holds the injected chat model, grounding context, and I/O seams; per gate it narrates the decision card, loops operator turns through the LLM, validates and confirms decisions, drives the resume, and persists the transcript.

nodes.name: validate_action_against_gate
nodes.summary: The load-bearing deterministic guard: checks a proposed verb and selection against the gate's allowed verb set before any engine drive, returning a ValidatedDecision or GuardRefusal.

nodes.name: _system_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds Marcus's persona system prompt anchored on the capability grounding context.

nodes.name: run_marcus_interlocutor
nodes.summary: Public entry that constructs a MarcusInterlocutor for a paused trial and runs the conversation loop to completion or clean error-pause.

nodes.name: _normalize_input_source
nodes.summary: Normalizes the injected operator input seam (callable, iterable, or interactive input) into a uniform line-yielding callable.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Interactive CLI entrypoint wiring real stdin/stdout, live chat model, freshly computed overlay staleness, and runtime preconditions into the interlocutor.

nodes.name: marcus_spoc.py
nodes.summary: Marcus SPOC — the conversational single-point-of-contact operator surface over the production engine: at each gate it narrates the relevant content in persona (sources, ingestion, lesson plan, research, treatment, voice, G0 enrichment), runs the styleguide picker preflight and pick-commit ceremony, collects per-gate decisions, and drives production_runner under the hood.
nodes.languageNotes: The deterministic orchestration engine remains production_runner; this module is the operator-facing narration layer, with decisions supplied as a gate_id-to-verb map (scripted or operator-in-the-loop).

nodes.name: _CeremonyBudget
nodes.summary: Small attempt-budget tracker for the picker confirm ceremony, consuming attempts until exhausted.

nodes.name: narrate_kickoff_beat1
nodes.summary: Renders Marcus's opening kickoff narration for a run: the beat-1 in-persona framing of what is about to be produced and from which sources.

nodes.name: _last_pick_for_course
nodes.summary: Scans the styleguide pick-event ledger for the most recent pick matching the course key, powering the pick recommendation narration.

nodes.name: narrate_research_result
nodes.summary: Narrates the research contribution outcome in persona, including degraded-mode disclosure when retrieval credentials were absent.

nodes.name: narrate_picker_publish_degrade
nodes.summary: Narrates a picker gh-pages publish failure honestly (with scrubbed error detail) and pivots the operator to the inline numbered fallback.

nodes.name: decode_and_echo_pick
nodes.summary: Decodes a pasted picker selection code against the roster and echoes the resolved style guide back to the operator for confirmation.

nodes.name: commit_picker_pick
nodes.summary: Commits a confirmed styleguide pick: validates it is pickable, writes the pick into the run directive, and appends the pick event to the ledger.

nodes.name: _confirm_codes
nodes.summary: Runs the confirm sub-loop of the picker ceremony: reads operator replies, distinguishes confirm/reject/re-paste, and enforces the ceremony budget.

nodes.name: _paste_confirm_commit_loop
nodes.summary: The paste-confirm-commit ceremony for the published picker page: accepts a pasted selection code, echoes the decode, and commits on operator confirmation.

nodes.name: _inline_numbered_pick
nodes.summary: Inline numbered-list fallback for styleguide selection when the published picker page is unavailable.

nodes.name: _degraded_publish_pick
nodes.summary: Full degraded-path pick flow when picker publish fails: narrates the degrade, offers the inline numbered list, and still routes the confirmed pick through the same commit path.

nodes.name: run_picker_preflight
nodes.summary: Pre-start styleguide picker ceremony: loads the roster, surfaces a last-pick recommendation, publishes the picker page (or degrades to inline pick), and commits the operator's confirmed choice into the directive.

nodes.name: build_operator_verdict_kwargs
nodes.summary: Translates a per-gate decision entry (verb plus optional edit payload/selection) into the kwargs for constructing an OperatorVerdict.

nodes.name: _narrate_g0_enrichment
nodes.summary: Narrates the G0 source-enrichment result (component enumeration, dissents, groundedness flags) at the G0E confirm gate.

nodes.name: narrate_gate
nodes.summary: Per-gate dispatcher choosing the right in-persona narration (sources, ingestion, lesson plan, research, treatment, voice, enrichment) for the decision card at the current pause.

nodes.name: run_plan_dialogue_preflight
nodes.summary: Optionally runs the interactive planning dialogue before trial start, threading its ratified artifacts into the run inputs.

nodes.name: run_marcus_spoc
nodes.summary: Main SPOC loop: starts the production trial, then at each gate pause narrates the decision card, resolves the mapped (or default approve) decision, and resumes production_runner until completion.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint parsing SPOC flags (corpus, decisions file, enrichment mode, picker options) and invoking run_marcus_spoc.

nodes.name: plan_dialogue_cli.py
nodes.summary: Interactive pre-start planning dialogue (Mine 2A): a thin REPL that elicits purpose, audience, workflow, gap-fill choice, and LOs, writes confirm-before-write ratification companions plus ratified-los.json, and persists a transcript — reusing the assess/ratify/write engine rather than rewriting it.

nodes.name: PlanDialogueSession
nodes.summary: The dialogue engine: injected-I/O elicitation of planning answers, confirm-before-write gating, ratification artifact plus ratified-LOs writing, and transcript persistence.

nodes.name: build_plan_dialogue_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse subtree for the plan-dialogue subcommand (corpus dir, output dir, scripted-answers file).

nodes.name: _load_script
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a scripted-answers file (JSON/YAML) into the deterministic input source used for non-interactive dialogue runs.

nodes.name: run_plan_dialogue
nodes.summary: Programmatic entry constructing a PlanDialogueSession over the assessed corpus and running elicitation through artifact writing.

nodes.name: plan_dialogue_cli
nodes.summary: argparse-facing handler wiring parsed flags (interactive or scripted input) into run_plan_dialogue and reporting written artifacts.

nodes.name: plan_ratify_cli.py
nodes.summary: Thin CLI adapter over the planning engine: assesses the source corpus or input bundle, ratifies the planning decision (workflow, gap-fill tradeoff, collateral), and writes the ratification artifacts.

nodes.name: build_plan_ratify_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse subtree for plan-ratify: corpus/bundle inputs, workflow and gap-fill choices, considered-alternatives, and collateral spec options.

nodes.name: _parse_considered
nodes.summary: Parses the considered-alternatives CLI inputs into structured GapFillTradeoff entries.

nodes.name: _load_collateral
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a CollateralSpec from a YAML/JSON file argument.

nodes.name: _assess
nodes.summary: Runs the source assessment, from either a built lesson-planning input bundle or a raw corpus directory.

nodes.name: run_plan_ratify
nodes.summary: Programmatic entry: assess, ratify the planning decision, and write ratification artifacts, returning the written paths.

nodes.name: plan_ratify_cli
nodes.summary: argparse-facing handler translating parsed flags into run_plan_ratify and reporting results.

nodes.name: trial.py
nodes.summary: CLI shim for production-clone trial launches: composes the run directive from the lesson corpus, runs the operator confirm-or-edit loop and picker preflight, then starts, resumes, recovers, or batch-resumes production trials via production_runner.
nodes.languageNotes: start_trial is a 300-line orchestration spine covering directive composition, editor-based confirmation, picker-pick preservation checks, and G0 enrichment prompts before handing the composed run to production_runner.

nodes.name: _resolve_editor
nodes.summary: Resolves the operator's editor command (env vars, sensible fallbacks) for the directive edit step, raising EditorUnavailableError when none is usable.

nodes.name: _confirm_or_edit_directive
nodes.summary: The operator confirm-or-edit loop over the composed directive: accept, open in editor and re-confirm, or decline (raising DirectiveDeclinedError).

nodes.name: _write_cancellation_record
nodes.summary: Persists a cancellation record when the operator declines the directive, keeping the aborted launch auditable.

nodes.name: _default_picker_preflight
nodes.summary: Default seam invoking the SPOC styleguide picker preflight before trial start; injectable so offline tests skip the live ceremony.

nodes.name: _warn_if_pick_dropped_by_edit
nodes.summary: Detects when an operator's directive edit dropped the committed styleguide pick and warns before the run proceeds without it.

nodes.name: start_trial
nodes.summary: The trial-launch spine: validates the lesson corpus leaf, composes and confirms the directive, runs picker and plan-dialogue preflights, resolves the component selection, applies gamma/LLM-mode settings, and starts the production trial.

nodes.name: _resolve_start_component_selection
nodes.summary: Resolves the ComponentSelection for a start: from an explicit bundle id, a ratified lesson-plan selection, or the front-door interactive pick.

nodes.name: start_trial_cli
nodes.summary: argparse-facing start handler mapping CLI flags into start_trial and rendering launch results and errors.

nodes.name: resume_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a paused trial from an OperatorVerdict file via resume_production_trial, resolving the run directory and verdict payload.

nodes.name: recover_trial
nodes.summary: Recovers an error-paused trial from its persisted checkpoint via recover_production_trial, replaying from the last durable state.

nodes.name: resume_batch_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a trial paused on a provider batch wait via resume_batch_production_trial (opt-in batch LLM execution mode).

nodes.name: build_trial_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the full argparse subtree for trial start/resume/recover/resume-batch, including directive, picker, enrichment, gamma-settings, and batch-mode flags.

nodes.name: canonical_processed_source.py
nodes.summary: Canonical processed-source contract and validators (Mine 4A): enforces the ratified lesson-leaf layout (slides/references/assessments), the run-dir layout (bundle/extracted.md, g0-enrichment.json), and the closed AssetKind shape-pin on enrichment nodes.

nodes.name: CanonicalValidationResult
nodes.summary: Accumulated validation outcome (errors, digests, checked paths) with raise_if_failed fail-loud semantics and a to_dict serialization.

nodes.name: resolve_asset_kind
nodes.summary: Maps a typed component's source type to its canonical AssetKind via the reconciliation table.

nodes.name: validate_lesson_leaf
nodes.summary: Validates a lesson-leaf directory against the greenlit slides/references/assessments layout, collecting digest evidence per file.

nodes.name: _enrichment_kind_errors
nodes.summary: Checks every enrichment node in g0-enrichment.json for a valid closed-set AssetKind, returning shape-pin violations.

nodes.name: validate_run_dir
nodes.summary: Validates a run directory: bundle/extracted.md presence, enrichment card presence, and enrichment kind shape-pins.

nodes.name: validate_canonical_tree
nodes.summary: Composite validator running lesson-leaf and run-dir validation as one canonical-tree check.

nodes.name: annotate_typed_components_with_kind
nodes.summary: Annotates typed components with their resolved AssetKind so downstream consumers read the closed kind directly.

nodes.name: write_canonical_contract_doc
nodes.summary: Emits the human-readable canonical-contract markdown documenting the enforced layout and kind rules.

nodes.name: facade.py
nodes.summary: The single Maya-facing Marcus facade: hides the Story 30-1 duality split behind one surface whose every Maya-visible string honors the binding voice register (first person, present tense, no hedges, no meta), with a module-level singleton via get_facade/reset_facade.
nodes.languageNotes: Internal routing identifiers never surface in Maya-visible strings; the one-voice rule is enforced by a dedicated leak test.

nodes.name: Facade
nodes.summary: The facade class exposing marcus_identity, run_step, ask, and run_4a as the one Marcus surface, internally routing to the manifest-compiled runtime, chat-model adapter, and run state.

nodes.name: _adhoc_trace_env
nodes.summary: Derives the LangSmith tracing environment context for ad-hoc facade asks.

nodes.name: _resolve_activation_allowlist
nodes.summary: Resolves which agent activations the facade permits, guarding the surface against unexpected routing.

nodes.name: _read_marcus_sanctum_digest
nodes.summary: Reads and fingerprints the Marcus sanctum artifacts so identity responses are bound to the actual persona state on disk.

nodes.name: get_facade
nodes.summary: Module-level singleton accessor constructing (once) and returning the shared Facade instance.

nodes.name: bundle_catalog.py
nodes.summary: Curated bundle catalog (S4): the 3 ratified bundles as frozen declarative records over ComponentSelection, each component carrying an honesty CapabilityTier seeded from current reality; bundle readiness is the worst-wins minimum tier the S5 front door uses to flag unproven bundles.
nodes.languageNotes: Closed sets (BundleId, CapabilityTier, BundleReadiness) are Pydantic-v2 closed-enum models wrapped in types.MappingProxyType; widening any set is a governance act, not a runtime flag.

nodes.name: ComponentCapability
nodes.summary: Per-component capability record binding a component to its honesty tier (proven_wired, proven_regressed_repairable, mechanism_only_never_produced, ...).

nodes.name: BundleRecord
nodes.summary: A blessed bundle: named ComponentSelection plus display metadata and required inputs, with a validator ensuring every selected component has a capability tier.

nodes.name: get_bundle
nodes.summary: Looks up a BundleRecord by its ratified bundle id from the frozen catalog.

nodes.name: min_capability_tier
nodes.summary: Computes the worst-wins minimum capability tier across a bundle's selected components.

nodes.name: bundle_readiness
nodes.summary: Maps a bundle's minimum capability tier to its front-door readiness verdict (fully_proven / partial / not_yet).

nodes.name: collateral_selection.py
nodes.summary: The selection edge from a ratified lesson-plan collateral intent to a curated ComponentSelection: reads the local ratified intent, resolves it through the bundle catalog, validates the bundle/collateral pairing, and returns the blessed selection for the production runner.

nodes.name: RatifiedLessonPlanCollateralIntent
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of the ratified collateral intent, with a validator rejecting remote source refs so only local ratified artifacts drive selection.

nodes.name: _bundle_from_intent
nodes.summary: Resolves the ratified intent's requested collateral to a catalog bundle id, defaulting to narrated-deck-with-motion when unspecified.

nodes.name: _validate_bundle_collateral_pair
nodes.summary: Asserts the resolved bundle actually delivers the collateral the intent asked for, failing loud on mismatches.

nodes.name: resolve_lesson_plan_collateral_selection
nodes.summary: Main resolver: takes a ratified collateral intent, resolves and validates the bundle, and returns the catalog's blessed ResolvedCollateralSelection.

nodes.name: load_lesson_plan_collateral_selection
nodes.summary: Loads a ratified intent file from disk and resolves it into the blessed ComponentSelection.

nodes.name: derive_selection_from_lesson_plan
nodes.summary: Derives the collateral selection directly from a ratified lesson-plan structure (asset plan items), mapping planned assets to catalog bundles.

nodes.name: load_selection_from_lesson_plan_json
nodes.summary: Loads a ratified lesson-plan JSON file and derives the blessed selection from it.

nodes.name: coverage_annotation.py
nodes.summary: The authored side of the coverage interlock (T4): builds one CoverageAnnotation per note-bearing component carrying re-segmented SourcePoint assertions, via a deterministic offline pass or a live gpt-5 segmentation leg where the LLM only cuts verbatim spans — risk flags and coverage intents are always assigned deterministically.
nodes.languageNotes: Live-leg harness discipline is T0-spike-proven: hard per-request OpenAI timeout with max_retries=0, generous max_completion_tokens, reasoning_effort=low; the module must not import g0_enrichment at runtime (layered via repin_additive instead).

nodes.name: CoverageAnnotation
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of a component's coverage annotation (source points, provenance, timestamps) with block invariants and a v1-shippability check.

nodes.name: NonVerbatimSpan
nodes.summary: Diagnostic record for a claimed verbatim span that does not actually appear in the source text after normalization.

nodes.name: detect_risk_flags
nodes.summary: Deterministic risk detector assigning flags (figures, numbers, citations, etc.) to a text span — never delegated to the LLM.

nodes.name: assert_v1_shippable
nodes.summary: Raises CoverageNotShippableError unless the annotation set meets the v1 shippability bar.

nodes.name: detect_incomplete_segmentation
nodes.summary: Finds source regions the segmentation pass failed to cover, returning IncompleteSegment diagnostics.

nodes.name: assert_segmentation_complete
nodes.summary: Raises CoverageIncompleteError with the offending segments when segmentation left source content uncovered.

nodes.name: reanchor_verbatim_span
nodes.summary: Re-anchors a claimed verbatim span onto the actual source text, tolerating quote-straightening and whitespace drift.

nodes.name: detect_non_verbatim_spans
nodes.summary: Scans annotations for spans that fail verbatim re-anchoring, emitting NonVerbatimSpan diagnostics since the model paraphrase is never trusted.

nodes.name: _point_from_span
nodes.summary: Converts a cut span into a SourcePoint with deterministic id, risk flags, coverage intents, and slide-key locator.

nodes.name: _annotation_from_spans
nodes.summary: Assembles a component's CoverageAnnotation from its cut spans, enforcing the per-block assertion ceiling.

nodes.name: extract_coverage_rows
nodes.summary: Extracts the note-bearing component rows (id, type, locator, excerpt, slide key) from the enrichment result that the coverage pass will segment.

nodes.name: build_coverage_from_rows
nodes.summary: Core builder running the offline or live segmentation over extracted rows and producing validated CoverageAnnotation objects with completeness checks.

nodes.name: render_live_segmentation_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the constrained gpt-5 segmentation prompt instructing the model to cut verbatim spans only.

nodes.name: _live_coverage_pass
nodes.summary: The live gpt-5 segmentation leg: per-request-timeout client, parses the model's span cuts, and re-anchors them verbatim before any use.

nodes.name: build_coverage_annotations
nodes.summary: Top-level entry mirroring build_pedagogy_annotations: extracts rows from the enrichment result and builds coverage annotations in the requested mode (offline default, live at T8).

nodes.name: coverage_gate.py
nodes.summary: Neutral fail-loud coverage gate logic (T6): blocks the run before any audio spend when a must-cover source point is missing from both deck and narration with no planned surface; fuzzy statuses stay warn/ledger-only during calibration.

nodes.name: CoverageAssuranceError
nodes.summary: Fail-loud coverage gate error; subclasses SpecialistDispatchError so the runner routes it through the existing _pause_at_error channel before audio spend.

nodes.name: _is_blocking
nodes.summary: Determines whether a receipt row is a deterministically-certain blocking failure (must-cover missing or verbatim-absent with no planned surface).

nodes.name: evaluate_coverage_gate
nodes.summary: Public gate-evaluation entry point returning the blocking rows for a derived coverage receipt.

nodes.name: coverage_warnings
nodes.summary: Collects warn-only ledger rows (altered/risky, low-confidence judgments) that do not halt the run during the calibration window.

nodes.name: evaluate_vacuous_receipt
nodes.summary: Detects vacuous receipts (no note-bearing rows or empty must-cover set) so an empty receipt cannot silently pass the gate.

nodes.name: assert_receipt_not_vacuous
nodes.summary: Raises CoverageAssuranceError when the derived receipt is vacuous.

nodes.name: assert_coverage_gate
nodes.summary: Raises CoverageAssuranceError with the blocking rows when the coverage gate fails; the single fail-loud enforcement surface.

nodes.name: coverage_receipt.py
nodes.summary: Derives the two-axis coverage receipt ledger — Axis A coverage (source to deliverable) and Axis B containment (deliverable to source) — from joins that already exist at the dispatch site, with deterministic locator-anchor resolution and figure-facet rows.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 models with extra="forbid" and validate_assignment per the project schema checklist; the two axes are joined per row but never merged into one status.

nodes.name: AnchorResolution
nodes.summary: Result of the deterministic locator-anchor resolution for a source point (anchor kind, matched slide key, confidence).

nodes.name: CoverageReceiptRow
nodes.summary: One receipt row joining a source point's coverage status (Axis A) and containment status (Axis B), kept as orthogonal fields.

nodes.name: CoverageReceipt
nodes.summary: The derived coverage-receipt.json artifact: full per-point accountability ledger with plan-view projections and shippability helpers.

nodes.name: _figure_token_set
nodes.summary: Extracts a normalized figure-token set from text for the figure-facet containment comparison.

nodes.name: _derive_must_cover
nodes.summary: Derives the must-cover flag for a source point from its coverage intents.

nodes.name: _derive_row
nodes.summary: Derives a full receipt row for one source point: resolves the deterministic anchor first, then projects coverage and containment statuses from existing joins.

nodes.name: _derive_figure_facet_row
nodes.summary: Derives the figure-facet sub-row verifying that source figures are actually carried into deck and narration surfaces.

nodes.name: _derive_containment
nodes.summary: Derives the Axis B containment status (verbatim_preserved/altered/risky) by comparing deliverable text back to the source span.

nodes.name: _resolve_anchor_for_point
nodes.summary: Resolves the deterministic locator anchor for a source point; no anchor forces status missing, regardless of any LLM judgment.

nodes.name: derive_coverage_receipt
nodes.summary: Top-level idempotent derivation of the CoverageReceipt from coverage annotations, anchors, and marshalled surfaces.

nodes.name: coverage_plan_view
nodes.summary: Projects the receipt into the operator-facing plan view grouped by slide.

nodes.name: coverage_plan_view_from_dicts
nodes.summary: Plan-view projection accepting raw dict rows loaded from a disk receipt.

nodes.name: g0_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models for the frozen, operator-confirmable G0 pre-pass result: the typed source manifest, candidate provisional learning objectives, enumeration provenance and traversal roots, plus the anti-anchoring artifacts (independent-parse sidecar and dissent ledger).
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the repo Pydantic-v2 checklist: ConfigDict(extra="forbid", validate_assignment=True) and timezone-aware datetimes; adequacy is deliberately absent (every provisional LO carries adequacy=None).

nodes.name: IndependentParse
nodes.summary: A4 anti-anchoring sidecar: Marcus's independent parse recorded before seeing the specialist output.

nodes.name: OperatorMerge
nodes.summary: Record of the operator's merge decision over the independent parse versus the specialist output.

nodes.name: Dissent
nodes.summary: A3 dissent-ledger entry capturing disagreement between parses with a resolution status.

nodes.name: ReconcileView
nodes.summary: Reconciliation view joining independent parse, specialist output, and dissents for operator confirmation at gate G0E.

nodes.name: CitationResolution
nodes.summary: Resolution record binding an extracted citation to a resolvable source location and resolution status.

nodes.name: G0EnrichmentResult
nodes.summary: The frozen result artifact of the G0 pre-pass: typed source manifest, provisional LOs, enumeration provenance, and anti-anchoring artifacts.

nodes.name: assert_run_dissent_invariant
nodes.summary: Asserts the run-level dissent invariant so unresolved dissents block operator confirmation.

nodes.name: corpus_fingerprint
nodes.summary: Computes the deterministic corpus fingerprint used as the cache and replay key for the frozen enrichment result.

nodes.name: irene_refinement.py
nodes.summary: Irene LO-refinement artifacts plus the signed LO-delta contract: refines provisional learning objectives in place on the same objective_id, attaches advisory source-adequacy alerts, and enforces count reconciliation with no silent drops for operator ratify-gate G0R.

nodes.name: LODeltaEntry
nodes.summary: One signed LO-delta ledger entry recording a per-objective change channel (refined text, Bloom level, adequacy) with digests.

nodes.name: LODeltaLedger
nodes.summary: The signed LO-delta contract: a reconcilable ledger of all refinement channels with count reconciliation and no silent drops.

nodes.name: IreneRefinementResult
nodes.summary: Frozen result artifact of Irene's LO refinement pass, re-presented to the operator at ratify-gate G0R.

nodes.name: _deterministic_adequacy
nodes.summary: Deterministic offline heuristic assigning an advisory SourceAdequacy alert for a learning objective.

nodes.name: refine_one
nodes.summary: Refines a single provisional LO in place (same objective_id) via the advance_lo transition guard with actor irene.

nodes.name: build_refinement_result
nodes.summary: Builds the full refinement result and delta ledger over all confirmed provisional LOs.

nodes.name: _live_refine
nodes.summary: Live-LLM refinement path producing refined LO statements and Bloom levels.

nodes.name: _parse_live_refinement
nodes.summary: Parses and validates the live refinement response into typed deltas, failing loud on malformed payloads.

nodes.name: assert_completeness
nodes.summary: Asserts count reconciliation across all ledger channels so every input LO is accounted for with no silent drops.

nodes.name: ratify_refined_los
nodes.summary: Applies the operator's G0R verdict, advancing refined LOs to ratified — the operator, never the model, advances status.

nodes.name: learning_objective.py
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for the canonical provenance-anchored LearningObjective entity with a lifecycle transition guard (provisional to refined to ratified), replacing three legacy in-code LO representations via adapter bridges.
nodes.languageNotes: Immutable objective_id carried across the whole lifecycle keys the learning_objective_map DB table; transitions are only legal through the advance_lo guard.

nodes.name: SourceRef
nodes.summary: Resolvable provenance reference binding a learning objective to its source component locations.

nodes.name: SourceAdequacy
nodes.summary: Advisory per-LO source-adequacy alert — never blocks the pipeline, forces a drop, or gates a status transition.

nodes.name: LearningObjective
nodes.summary: Canonical lifecycle-aware learning objective entity with immutable objective_id, provenance refs, and Bloom level.

nodes.name: advance_lo
nodes.summary: The single transition guard for LO status changes; raises IllegalTransition on out-of-order or wrong-actor transitions.

nodes.name: pedagogy_annotation.py
nodes.summary: P3 pedagogical annotation overlay: one additive, never-gating PedagogyAnnotation per annotatable typed component carrying Bloom level, pedagogical role, deterministic teaches_after ordering, a teachable verdict derived from front-matter status, and a bounded rationale — with both offline and live-LLM passes.

nodes.name: PedagogyAnnotation
nodes.summary: The P3 overlay model per annotatable component: Bloom level, pedagogical role, teaches_after ordering, teachable verdict, and bounded audit rationale.

nodes.name: is_annotatable
nodes.summary: Predicate deciding whether a typed component type receives a pedagogy annotation.

nodes.name: compute_teaches_after
nodes.summary: Computes the deterministic teaches_after ordering from document ordinals.

nodes.name: derive_teachable
nodes.summary: Derives the teachable verdict from the front-matter resolution_status, never from citation_resolutions.

nodes.name: assert_p2_frontmatter_present
nodes.summary: Asserts each component carries the required P2 universal-md front-matter fields before annotation.

nodes.name: assert_pedagogy_referential_invariant
nodes.summary: Asserts every annotation references an existing component and that its LO ids resolve.

nodes.name: assert_pedagogy_teachable_consistency
nodes.summary: Asserts teachable verdicts remain consistent with component resolution status.

nodes.name: _offline_pedagogy_pass
nodes.summary: Deterministic offline annotation pass used when live dispatch is off.

nodes.name: _extract_pedagogy_rows
nodes.summary: Extracts candidate annotation rows from the enrichment card payload.

nodes.name: _build_live_annotations
nodes.summary: Builds validated PedagogyAnnotation objects from parsed live-LLM rows with enum normalization and deterministic fallbacks.

nodes.name: _live_pedagogy_pass
nodes.summary: Live-LLM annotation pass: renders the prompt, dispatches the call, and parses the response.

nodes.name: _render_live_pedagogy_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the live pedagogy prompt embedding component front matter and the closed enums.

nodes.name: build_pedagogy_annotations
nodes.summary: Entry point choosing the offline or live pass and returning the validated annotation set.

nodes.name: planning_context.py
nodes.summary: Loads optional planning context (purpose, audience, learning-objective briefs, assessment) by merging on-disk planning-ratification.json and ratified-los.json under a run directory, and assesses LO coverage of plan units; absent artifacts yield None so default Irene behavior is preserved.

nodes.name: PlanningContext
nodes.summary: Optional planning frame (purpose, audience, LO briefs, assessment) merged from ratification artifacts for Irene Pass-1 emphasis.

nodes.name: _los_from_ratified
nodes.summary: Loads learning-objective briefs from the on-disk ratified-los.json artifact.

nodes.name: _from_ratification
nodes.summary: Builds a PlanningContext from planning-ratification.json content.

nodes.name: _significant_tokens
nodes.summary: Tokenizes text into significant, stopword-filtered tokens for LO-coverage matching.

nodes.name: assess_lo_coverage
nodes.summary: Assesses how well plan units cover each ratified LO via significant-token overlap scoring, producing a coverage receipt.

nodes.name: assert_lo_coverage_or_fail
nodes.summary: Raises PlanningContextError when LO coverage falls below the acceptance threshold.

nodes.name: load_planning_context
nodes.summary: Loads the optional planning context from a run directory; absent artifacts return None.

nodes.name: planning_ratification.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight planning ratification recorder for the Phase-2 selection bridge: captures purpose, audience, plan choices, and gap-fill tradeoffs, then emits a canonical RatifiedLessonPlanCollateralIntent onto the existing selection edge.

nodes.name: GapFillTradeoff
nodes.summary: Records one gap-fill tradeoff decision: the gap, the chosen approach, and its rationale.

nodes.name: PlanningRatificationRecord
nodes.summary: Canonical record of the operator's planning ratification: purpose, audience, plan choices, tradeoffs, and workflow.

nodes.name: SelectionDelta
nodes.summary: Delta between the baseline and the ratified component selections.

nodes.name: _intent_for_workflow
nodes.summary: Maps the ratified workflow to the canonical S8 RatifiedLessonPlanCollateralIntent.

nodes.name: _assert_gap_fill_consistent_with_workflow
nodes.summary: Validates that gap-fill tradeoffs are consistent with the chosen workflow.

nodes.name: ratify_planning_decision
nodes.summary: Builds and validates the ratification record and derives the collateral intent for the selection edge.

nodes.name: write_ratification_artifacts
nodes.summary: Writes planning-ratification.json and its companion view into the run directory.

nodes.name: compute_selection_delta
nodes.summary: Computes the SelectionDelta between the baseline and ratified selections.

nodes.name: run_asset_index.py
nodes.summary: UDAC v1 Run Asset Index (RAI): the neutral access-and-use contract with canonical digesting, typed legacy modes, fail-loud loaders, and asset resolution to a read-only view — placed models-side so both the orchestrator (builder) and specialists (consumers) can import it without violating the M3 layering contract.
nodes.languageNotes: RESOLVE-ASSET-LIVES-NEUTRAL discipline: this module imports no app.marcus.orchestrator module so specialists can consume the resolved view without a layering violation.

nodes.name: RunAssetEntry
nodes.summary: One RAI entry: asset id, path, digest, authority status, and declared usage.

nodes.name: ResolvedAsset
nodes.summary: Read-only resolved view of an asset handed to consuming specialists.

nodes.name: RunAssetIndex
nodes.summary: The Run Asset Index built by the orchestrator at ratification gates and consumed by specialists as the single access contract.

nodes.name: recompute_digest_from_disk
nodes.summary: Recomputes an asset digest from disk, honoring the typed legacy mode and digest algorithm.

nodes.name: compare_digests
nodes.summary: Compares recorded versus recomputed digests, classifying match, mismatch, and legacy outcomes.

nodes.name: resolve_asset
nodes.summary: Neutral asset resolution: validates authority and digest and returns a ResolvedAsset; raises AssetResolutionError on any contract breach.

nodes.name: load_rai
nodes.summary: Fail-loud loader for the Run Asset Index from a run directory.

nodes.name: write_rai
nodes.summary: Writes the canonical RAI JSON artifact (orchestrator-side writer, separated from loaders).

nodes.name: source_assessment.py
nodes.summary: Assesses available source material for lesson-planning ratification as a thin adapter over course-source input bundles and curated corpus directory leaves; performs no remote ingestion and fills no missing media.

nodes.name: SourceAssessment
nodes.summary: Assessment summary of available source material (kinds, counts, gap summary) for planning ratification.

nodes.name: assess_from_input_bundle
nodes.summary: Builds a SourceAssessment from a lesson-planning input bundle.

nodes.name: assess_from_corpus_dir
nodes.summary: Builds a SourceAssessment by scanning a curated corpus directory on disk.

nodes.name: assessments_differ_in_kind_or_count
nodes.summary: Detects material differences between two assessments (source kind or count changes).

nodes.name: source_point.py
nodes.summary: SourcePoint model — the atomic teaching-assertion unit of the coverage axis, produced by re-segmenting a slide's presentation-note block; a child sub-locator of an existing TypedComponent whose only sanctioned join surface is the parent component_id.

nodes.name: SourcePoint
nodes.summary: Atomic teaching-assertion unit of the coverage axis with verbatim span, assertion kind, and coverage intents; id is component_id#ordinal.

nodes.name: verbatim_required_for
nodes.summary: Policy predicate deciding whether a point's assertion kind requires verbatim preservation.

nodes.name: make_source_point_id
nodes.summary: Constructs the child id component_id#ordinal — a sub-locator, never a join key.

nodes.name: join_key
nodes.summary: The only sanctioned join surface: returns the parent component_id for all downstream joins.

nodes.name: assert_join_key
nodes.summary: Asserts a join uses the parent component id, rejecting #ordinal child ids as join keys.

nodes.name: derive_coverage_intents
nodes.summary: Derives per-point coverage intents (must_cover, verbatim) from the assertion kind and note content.

nodes.name: source_type.py
nodes.summary: Closed 10-type source taxonomy (plus an other:<label> escape hatch) with TypedSource and TypedComponent models for the G0 enrichment brick; TYPE is recorded independently of the operator directive role and the two are never overloaded onto one field.

nodes.name: OtherSourceType
nodes.summary: Escape-hatch type other:<label> for spans outside the closed 10-type taxonomy, surfaced flagged rather than consumed.

nodes.name: TypedSource
nodes.summary: A typed source-file record whose TYPE classification is independent of the operator directive role.

nodes.name: TypedComponent
nodes.summary: An intra-document typed component with document-hierarchy locator and verbatim excerpt.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry for the Marcus orchestrator (4A conversation loop, plan-lock commit, downstream fan-out): re-exports routing and supervisor surfaces and defines the NegotiatorSeam; external callers use the Marcus facade instead of importing this package directly.

nodes.name: NegotiatorSeam
nodes.summary: Seam object exposing the negotiation surface of the 4A conversation loop to the supervisor.

nodes.name: chooser_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Publishes the per-slide Storyboard-A A/B chooser page to GitHub Pages at gate G2B: renders the chooser HTML from Gary's per-slide PNGs and pushes a self-contained pack so the operator can pick variants slide-by-slide; the selection code returns as the G2B verdict.

nodes.name: _per_slide_options
nodes.summary: Collects the A/B variant options (per-slide PNGs and metadata) from Gary's dispatched outputs.

nodes.name: publish_chooser_for_gate
nodes.summary: Renders the per-slide A/B chooser HTML pack and publishes it to gh-pages for the G2B pick.

nodes.name: _git_publish_dir
nodes.summary: Delegates the clone-stage-commit-push transport to the shared gh-pages publish helper.

nodes.name: conversation_persistence.py
nodes.summary: Persists Marcus conversation turns with a tamper-evident SHA256 hash chain: canonical JSON digesting, chained turn writes, ordered loading, and full chain verification that fails loud on breaks.

nodes.name: canonical_json_bytes
nodes.summary: Serializes a payload to canonical sorted JSON bytes for stable digesting.

nodes.name: compute_turn_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA256 chain digest of a turn over its canonical bytes plus the previous turn digest.

nodes.name: latest_turn_digest
nodes.summary: Returns the digest of the most recent persisted turn for chain continuation.

nodes.name: load_turn
nodes.summary: Loads a persisted conversation turn from disk.

nodes.name: write_turn
nodes.summary: Persists a conversation turn with its chained digest, decision card, and monotonic index ordering.

nodes.name: verify_chain
nodes.summary: Verifies the tamper-evident digest chain across all persisted turns, raising ConversationChainBrokenError on any break.

nodes.name: coverage_anchors.py
nodes.summary: Pure, offline-testable core for the deck side of the coverage join: reconciles final slide ids to source slide_keys from the slide_briefs source_ref lineage and projects deck and narration text anchors; drifted briefs are omitted, never guessed.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately replicates minimal loader logic instead of cross-importing app.specialists, keeping the M3 layering boundary clean (mirrors Irene's loader-replication discipline).

nodes.name: resolve_slide_key_map
nodes.summary: Builds the primary {final_slide_id: slide_key} map from the slide_briefs source_ref to plan_units lineage; drifted briefs are omitted, never guessed.

nodes.name: resolve_slide_key_lineage
nodes.summary: Resolves the full slide-key lineage, including clustered sub-slides that share one source key.

nodes.name: _union_text
nodes.summary: Unions a slide's deck text surfaces into a single anchor text.

nodes.name: build_coverage_anchors
nodes.summary: Pure reconciliation core projecting deck and narration text anchors keyed by slide_key for the coverage join.

nodes.name: coverage_gate_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator wiring for the coverage gate (T6): env feature flag (default OFF), disk-primary receipt I/O, and the both-walks seam callable invoked at the shared dispatch site to enforce the gate before any audio-spending specialist runs.

nodes.name: coverage_gate_active
nodes.summary: Env-flag predicate for the coverage gate; default OFF keeps the existing pipeline byte-identical.

nodes.name: mark_coverage_receipt_expected
nodes.summary: Drops the receipt-expected marker first so a later derive failure still trips the fail-loud gate instead of silently bypassing it.

nodes.name: write_coverage_receipt
nodes.summary: Writes the derived coverage receipt to its disk-primary path in the run directory.

nodes.name: note_bearing_content_exists
nodes.summary: Checks whether the run's enrichment carries note-bearing content that makes the coverage gate applicable.

nodes.name: enforce_coverage_gate_before_audio
nodes.summary: Both-walks seam callable: loads the receipt from disk and asserts the coverage gate before audio-spending specialist dispatch.

nodes.name: coverage_runner.py
nodes.summary: Runner-integration glue for the G3 coverage seam: marshals the run's own deck and narration export surfaces into anchors, derives and writes the coverage receipt as a canonical RAI artifact, and renders the operator-facing coverage report HTML — via one shared helper called at both walk sites.

nodes.name: _load_coverage_annotations
nodes.summary: Loads the authored coverage annotations from the frozen g0-enrichment.json artifact.

nodes.name: _select_plan_units
nodes.summary: Selects the lesson-plan units in scope for the run's coverage join.

nodes.name: _collision_aware_slide_key_map
nodes.summary: Builds the slide-key map while detecting and disambiguating breadcrumb-ordinal collisions.

nodes.name: _marshal_coverage_surfaces
nodes.summary: Marshals the run's own deck rows and narration segments (run-dir export SSOT) into coverage anchors.

nodes.name: _write_coverage_report_html
nodes.summary: Renders and writes the operator-facing coverage report HTML via the storyboard emitter.

nodes.name: _derive_and_write_coverage_receipt
nodes.summary: The one shared G3 helper both walk sites call: drops the expected marker first, derives the receipt, writes the RAI artifact, and renders the report.

nodes.name: dispatch_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Production composition adapter for scaffolded specialists: composes specialist graphs behind the manifest-compiled run graph, resolving builders dynamically and marshalling run state, cache state, and production-envelope contributions across dispatch.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LangGraph InMemorySaver checkpointing and importlib-based dynamic builder resolution against the manifest compiler's SPECIALIST_ALIASES.

nodes.name: ProductionDispatchAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter that composes scaffolded specialist graphs behind the manifest-compiled production graph, marshalling run/cache state and returning envelope contributions.

nodes.name: enrichment_consumption.py
nodes.summary: P5-S2 orchestrator-side consumption layer projecting the frozen, operator-confirmed G0 enrichment card into the two learner-facing seams: per-slide role-derived voice-direction seeds for narration (Irene Pass-2) and a sanitized deck-enrichment hint for the deck composer.

nodes.name: deck_enrichment_active
nodes.summary: Env-flag predicate for deck-side enrichment consumption.

nodes.name: role_to_voice_direction
nodes.summary: Maps a pedagogical role to its frozen voice_direction default via the PEDAGOGICAL_ROLE_TO_VOICE table.

nodes.name: source_slide_ordinals
nodes.summary: Extracts the source slide ordinals a component teaches from its document locator.

nodes.name: project_role_derived_voice_by_slide
nodes.summary: Consumer A: projects role-derived voice-direction seeds keyed by slide ordinal for the narration specialist.

nodes.name: _candidate_narration_seeds_by_ordinal
nodes.summary: Collects candidate narration role seeds per slide ordinal, dropping ambiguous 0-or-many matches.

nodes.name: project_ambiguous_narration_ordinals
nodes.summary: Reports slide ordinals whose narration seed match was ambiguous, so drops are logged rather than silent.

nodes.name: project_deck_enrichment_hint
nodes.summary: Consumer B: projects a sanitized deck-enrichment hint statement for the deck composer.

nodes.name: g0_enrichment_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Runner-wired G0 pre-pass orchestration: enumerates the corpus, segments every source file into typed components with locators and verbatim excerpts, extracts provisional LOs offline or via a live-LLM pass, caches the frozen result keyed to a corpus fingerprint, and attaches pedagogy/coverage front matter for operator confirm-gate G0E.
nodes.languageNotes: The live path includes truncated-JSON salvage parsing so a cut-off LLM payload degrades to a recoverable parse error rather than silent data loss.

nodes.name: resolve_enrichment_mode
nodes.summary: Resolves the enrichment mode (off, offline, live) from environment flags and the run directive.

nodes.name: g0_enrichment_active
nodes.summary: Predicate for whether the G0 pre-pass runs at all.

nodes.name: _split_components
nodes.summary: Segments one source file's text into N typed components, each with a document-hierarchy locator and verbatim excerpt.

nodes.name: _file_components
nodes.summary: Produces the typed component set for a single enumerated corpus file.

nodes.name: flag_ungrounded_components
nodes.summary: Flags components whose excerpt is not grounded in the normalized source text — the anti-hallucination groundedness check.

nodes.name: _offline_pre_pass
nodes.summary: Deterministic offline pre-pass building the typed manifest and provisional LOs without any LLM.

nodes.name: _build_dissent
nodes.summary: Builds A3 dissent-ledger entries where the independent parse and the specialist output disagree.

nodes.name: _parse_live_extraction_response
nodes.summary: Parses the live extraction response, salvaging truncated JSON payloads and failing loud on unrecoverable ones.

nodes.name: _live_pre_pass
nodes.summary: Live-LLM pre-pass dispatching the extraction prompt over the corpus summary.

nodes.name: _parse_live_payload
nodes.summary: Validates and coerces live payload rows into typed components and provisional learning objectives.

nodes.name: build_enrichment_result
nodes.summary: Assembles the frozen G0EnrichmentResult (manifest, LOs, provenance, dissent) with corpus-fingerprint caching for replay determinism.

nodes.name: run_g0_enrichment
nodes.summary: Runner-wired entry point: enumerates the corpus, runs the pre-pass, attaches pedagogy and coverage front matter, and freezes the result to the run directory.

nodes.name: load_enrichment_result
nodes.summary: Loads the frozen g0-enrichment.json result from a run directory.

nodes.name: gate_runner.py
nodes.summary: Shared gate-runner guardrails from the Slab 7a closeout: calibration-tripwire evaluation over gate verdict history, engagement-decay reporting, and Marcus duality-boundary assertions keeping orchestrator and persona sides separated.

nodes.name: evaluate_calibration_tripwire
nodes.summary: Evaluates the calibration tripwire over gate verdict history, flagging drift beyond configured thresholds.

nodes.name: emit_engagement_decay_report
nodes.summary: Emits the engagement-decay JSONL report across gate interactions.

nodes.name: assert_marcus_duality_boundary
nodes.summary: Asserts the Marcus duality boundary — orchestrator-side code never crosses into persona-side surfaces.

nodes.name: assert_payload_duality_boundary
nodes.summary: Asserts payloads crossing the duality boundary carry no forbidden fields.

nodes.name: gh_pages_publish.py
nodes.summary: Shared gh-pages publish helper consolidating the clone-stage-commit-push transport with three fail-loud hygiene guards — retention pruning by git committer date, a published-size guard against the GitHub Pages 1 GB cap, and post-push build verification — so the public asset host can never silently freeze again.

nodes.name: _sanitize_pack
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a pack directory before publish: filename hygiene checks and token scrubbing.

nodes.name: prune_retention
nodes.summary: Deletes managed packs older than max_age_days by git committer date, honoring the protected allowlist and never touching the pack being published.

nodes.name: project_published_size
nodes.summary: Size guard: sums the checked-out working tree and raises SizeGuardRefusal before pushing past the Pages published-site cap.

nodes.name: _verify_page_live
nodes.summary: Polls the published URL until the new content is live or the timeout budget is exhausted.

nodes.name: verify_build_after_push
nodes.summary: Verifies the GitHub Pages build succeeded after push via the Pages build API.

nodes.name: git_publish_dir
nodes.summary: The consolidated clone, stage, commit, and push transport with the hygiene guards woven in.

nodes.name: load_hygiene_config
nodes.summary: Loads the retention, size-guard, and verify hygiene configuration for the publish helpers.

nodes.name: irene_refinement_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Runner-wired Irene refinement hook (Story G0-S3): consumes gate-#1-confirmed provisional LOs from the frozen enrichment artifact, refines each in place with Bloom level and advisory adequacy, freezes the signed LO-delta contract to irene-refinement.json, and feeds operator ratify-gate G0R.

nodes.name: irene_refinement_active
nodes.summary: Env-flag predicate for the Irene refinement brick.

nodes.name: _load_confirmed_provisional_los
nodes.summary: Reads the gate-#1-confirmed provisional LOs from the frozen g0-enrichment.json.

nodes.name: build_result_for_run
nodes.summary: Builds the refinement result for a run from its confirmed provisional LOs.

nodes.name: run_irene_refinement
nodes.summary: Runner-wired hook: refines confirmed LOs (offline or live), freezes the signed LO-delta contract to the run directory, and feeds gate G0R.

nodes.name: load_refinement_result
nodes.summary: Loads the frozen refinement result from a run directory.

nodes.name: load_refinement_full
nodes.summary: Loads the full refinement payload including the delta ledger and adequacy channels.

nodes.name: package_builders.py
nodes.summary: S3 pre-dispatch package builders: pure, deterministic transforms projecting upstream envelope contributions (ratified lesson plan plus creative directive) into the exact payload vocabulary a downstream specialist consumes — no LLM in the deterministic neck, invoked at their manifest node like pre_gate_marcus.

nodes.name: BuilderInputError
nodes.summary: Fail-loud error raised for missing or malformed upstream contributions at a builder node.

nodes.name: _unit_included
nodes.summary: Predicate for whether a plan unit is included in the dispatch scope.

nodes.name: build_gary_briefs
nodes.summary: Pure deterministic projection of the ratified lesson plan and creative directive into Gary's per-slide brief payload vocabulary.

nodes.name: _deck_enrichment_hint
nodes.summary: Fetches the sanitized deck-enrichment hint from the enrichment consumption layer.

nodes.name: run_builder_node
nodes.summary: Executes a builder at its manifest node and lands the output as a first-class envelope contribution keyed to that node.

nodes.name: picker_html_emitter.py
nodes.summary: Renders the static gh-pages styleguide picker page and decodes the SGP selection code the operator pastes back into marcus_spoc. Return-path twin of the chooser HTML emitter; roster loading and card CSS are imported from styleguide_picker so the two transports cannot drift, and the selection-code grammar is frozen in one place shared by the embedded JS encoder and the Python decoder.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds a full client-side JavaScript encoder as a Python string so the static page stays dependency-free and inert (write-locus stays on the marcus side).

nodes.name: build_selection_code
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical SGP-{run_tag}-A:{slug}[ B:{slug}] selection code string from a run tag and pick map, the reference implementation of the frozen grammar.

nodes.name: decode_picker_selection_code
nodes.summary: Parses and validates a pasted selection code against the expected run tag and SSOT slug membership, returning the {A: slug, B: slug?} pick map or failing loud on grammar, tag, or lifecycle violations.

nodes.name: _page_js
nodes.summary: Emits the embedded client-side JavaScript that lets the operator pick styleguides and versions and builds the selection code in the browser, mirroring the Python grammar exactly.

nodes.name: render_picker_static_html
nodes.summary: Renders the complete dependency-free static picker page (roster cards, thumbnails, embedded JS encoder) that gets published to the public gh-pages site.

nodes.name: picker_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Publishes the static styleguide picker page plus roster thumbnails to the public GitHub Pages site as a self-contained pack. Thin caller over the shared gh_pages_publish transport that adds the PickerPublishError family, retention pruning, size guard, and verify-after-push so the public site never grows past the GitHub Pages 1 GB cap.

nodes.name: publish_picker
nodes.summary: Builds the picker pack (index.html plus thumbnails), runs sanitation, retention prune and size guard, pushes it to the gh-pages site, and optionally verifies the page is live after push; fail-loud on missing token or git failure.

nodes.name: _git_publish_dir
nodes.summary: Delegates the clone/copy/commit/push of a local pack directory into the public site repo to the shared gh_pages_publish transport, wrapping failures in PickerPublishError.

nodes.name: pre_gate_marcus.py
nodes.summary: Shared pre-gate LLM node invoked once before each conversational pause point (G1, G2C, G3, G4): renders a Jinja2 template-with-slots prompt per gate, calls the marcus chat model, and parses a structured PreFillProposal that the runner threads into the decision card's drafted proposal.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 StrictUndefined so any missing template slot fails loud at render time instead of emitting a silently incomplete prompt.

nodes.name: PreFillProposal
nodes.summary: Dataclass carrying the structured pre-fill output of the pre-gate LLM call (proposal text plus metadata) that gets threaded into decision-card drafting.

nodes.name: render_pre_fill_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the gate-specific Jinja2 template from docs/conversational-gates with the supplied slot values into the pre-fill prompt text.

nodes.name: invoke_pre_gate_marcus
nodes.summary: One LLM call site per gate: renders the slot prompt, invokes the marcus chat model factory, and returns the parsed PreFillProposal without ever promoting a gate from non-blocking to blocking.

nodes.name: _parse_pre_fill_response
nodes.summary: Parses the model's JSON response content into a PreFillProposal, tolerating fenced code blocks and failing loud on malformed payloads.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02A LLM-driven directive composer, re-exporting the composer entry points (compose, write_directive_yaml), the ComposerCache, and the Directive model family as the public API.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using explicit __all__ to pin the public surface.

nodes.name: _cache.py
nodes.summary: Minimal in-memory response cache for the Section 02A composer, mapping normalized-prompt cache keys to LLM classification responses so equivalent prompts reuse prior answers.

nodes.name: ComposerCache
nodes.summary: Dict-backed get/set cache keyed by SHA256 prompt digests, letting the composer skip repeated LLM invocations for identical classification prompts.

nodes.name: _prompt.py
nodes.summary: Prompt rendering and cache-key normalization for Section 02A: renders the Jinja2 corpus-classification prompt and strips volatile identity (operator_id, timestamps, UUID4 run_ids) before SHA256-hashing so semantically equal prompts share a cache key.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 with StrictUndefined against a template under docs/conversational-gates, and regex-based volatile-token stripping for deterministic cache keys.

nodes.name: render_classification_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the section-02a-composer.j2 template into a per-file classification prompt, including file metadata, corpus summary, and a hex sample for binary files.

nodes.name: normalize_prompt
nodes.summary: Strips volatile tokens (operator ids, ISO timestamps, UUID4 values) from a prompt via regex substitution so cache keys are stable across runs.

nodes.name: cache_key_for_prompt
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA256 hex digest of the normalized prompt, producing the cache key used by ComposerCache.

nodes.name: corpus_summary_for
nodes.summary: Builds a newline-joined listing of corpus-relative file paths used as shared context inside every classification prompt.

nodes.name: cli_adapter.py
nodes.summary: CLI-side adapter bridging the Section 02A composer's compose/write_directive_yaml API to the legacy path/digest call shape expected by trial CLI callers, including model resolution trail persistence and directive SHA256 digest computation.

nodes.name: compose_and_write
nodes.summary: Validates the corpus directory, resolves or accepts a chat model, writes a model_resolution_trail.json audit record, runs compose, writes the directive YAML, and returns the path plus its SHA256 digest.

nodes.name: composer.py
nodes.summary: Core LLM-driven Section 02A directive composition: walks a lesson corpus leaf, applies rule-based exclusions, classifies each remaining file via a cached LangChain chat-model call, and assembles a validated Directive that can be serialized to YAML.
nodes.languageNotes: Fail-loud design: raises NonRunnableScopeError when pointed at a course/module container root instead of a lesson corpus leaf, enforcing the lesson-scoped corpus contract.

nodes.name: DirectiveCompositionError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError subclass raised when the Section 02A corpus cannot be composed (missing directory, malformed LLM payload, empty corpus).

nodes.name: NonRunnableScopeError
nodes.summary: DirectiveCompositionError subclass raised when composition is pointed at a course/module container root rather than a lesson corpus leaf.

nodes.name: assert_lesson_corpus_leaf
nodes.summary: Fails loudly with NonRunnableScopeError when the corpus directory contains course/module sentinel markers (course.yaml, module.yaml, modules/), guarding against running composition at container scope.

nodes.name: _walk_corpus_files
nodes.summary: Validates the corpus directory exists and is a lesson leaf, then returns a sorted recursive listing of all files under it.

nodes.name: _source_from_payload
nodes.summary: Constructs a DirectiveSource from a parsed LLM classification payload, mapping role, description, and expected_min_words fields.

nodes.name: _ignored_source
nodes.summary: Builds a DirectiveSource marked with an excluded_reason for files rejected by rule-based exclusion, preserving them in the directive for auditability.

nodes.name: _classify_with_llm
nodes.summary: Classifies a single corpus file: renders the classification prompt, checks the ComposerCache by normalized cache key, invokes the chat model on a miss, caches the response, and parses it into a DirectiveSource.

nodes.name: compose
nodes.summary: Top-level composition entry point: walks the corpus, applies rule-based exclusions, LLM-classifies remaining files with per-run ref_ids, and assembles a timezone-aware, UUID4-stamped Directive.

nodes.name: write_directive_yaml
nodes.summary: Serializes a Directive to YAML via model_dump and yaml.safe_dump, creating parent directories and returning the written path.

nodes.name: directive_model.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model family for the Section 02A directive: role and excluded-reason vocabularies, the per-file DirectiveSource with role/field invariants and legacy-key migration, and the top-level Directive with UUID4 and timezone-aware validators.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the repo's Pydantic v2 checklist: closed-enum rejection via TypeAdapter literal validation, enforce_uuid4_version/enforce_tz_aware field validators, and a model_dump override that excludes internal fields.

nodes.name: DirectiveRole
nodes.summary: Closed vocabulary of directive source roles used to classify corpus files within a Section 02A directive.

nodes.name: ExcludedReason
nodes.summary: Closed vocabulary of reasons a corpus file may be excluded from directive composition (e.g., rule-based operational-file exclusion).

nodes.name: DirectiveSource
nodes.summary: Per-file directive entry carrying ref_id, locator, provider, role, description, and expected_min_words; validators migrate the legacy source_id key, reject unknown roles/excluded reasons, and enforce role-conditional field invariants.

nodes.name: Directive
nodes.summary: Top-level Section 02A directive model with run_id (enforced UUID4), corpus_dir, composed sources, timezone-aware composed_at, schema_version, and gamma_settings; overrides model_dump to trim internal fields.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02A G0 poll surface, re-exporting SURFACE_ID and the directive display, edit, verdict-submission, and resume helpers.

nodes.name: _transports.py
nodes.summary: In-process transport handlers (CLI, HTTP, MCP-stdio) for the Section 02A G0 poll surface; each wraps the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns a uniform transport-tagged response with directive display, verdict, and resume result.
nodes.languageNotes: The three transports intentionally share one code path (_transport_response), keeping CLI/HTTP/MCP behavior in parity by construction.

nodes.name: _transport_response
nodes.summary: Shared response builder that packages the transport name, directive display card, verdict dump, and resume_from_verdict result into one payload.

nodes.name: submit_cli_verdict
nodes.summary: CLI transport entry: forwards an operator verdict (approve/edit/reject) into the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns the uniform transport response.

nodes.name: submit_http_verdict
nodes.summary: HTTP transport entry: forwards an operator verdict into the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns the uniform transport response.

nodes.name: submit_mcp_stdio_verdict
nodes.summary: MCP-stdio transport entry: forwards an operator verdict into the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns the uniform transport response.

nodes.name: poll_surface.py
nodes.summary: Canonical Section 02A G0 poll-surface: loads and displays directives with canonical SHA256 decision-card digests, applies field-level operator edits to whitelisted DirectiveSource fields, validates verdict submission, and resumes runs only when the verdict digest matches the (possibly edited) directive.
nodes.languageNotes: Registers a parity contract mandating cli/http/mcp-stdio transports; digest matching over canonical JSON bytes prevents resuming from a stale or tampered decision card.

nodes.name: canonical_model_bytes
nodes.summary: Serializes a Pydantic model to canonical JSON bytes (sorted keys, compact separators, ASCII) so digests are stable across processes.

nodes.name: compute_model_digest
nodes.summary: Returns the SHA256 hex digest of a model's canonical bytes; used as the decision-card digest binding verdicts to directive content.

nodes.name: load_directive
nodes.summary: Reads a directive YAML file and validates it into a Directive model via model_validate.

nodes.name: display_directive
nodes.summary: Produces the operator-facing decision card: the directive dump paired with its computed decision-card digest and surface id.

nodes.name: apply_directive_edit
nodes.summary: Applies a DirectiveEditPayload to a directive: resolves each ref_id to its source row, rejects unknown ref_ids and non-whitelisted fields, applies updates, and re-validates the whole Directive, wrapping validation failures in GateError.

nodes.name: _directive_for_verdict
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective directive for a verdict verb: the original for approve/reject, the edited directive for edit (requiring an edit payload).

nodes.name: submit_verdict
nodes.summary: Builds a validated Section02AOperatorVerdict for approve/edit/reject: coerces the directive, normalizes the edit payload, computes the effective directive, and stamps the decision-card digest of what the operator approved.

nodes.name: resume_from_verdict
nodes.summary: Gate-resume guard: recomputes the effective directive's digest and refuses to resume (GateError) on run_id or digest mismatch, returning the resumed directive and verdict dumps only when the verdict provably matches.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_02a.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 models for Section 02A HIL poll-surface verdicts: DirectiveEditPayload (field edits keyed by source ref_id) and Section02AOperatorVerdict with UUID4, timezone-aware, SHA256-digest, and verb/payload consistency validators.
nodes.languageNotes: Verb/payload consistency is enforced at model level: edit requires an edit_payload, reject requires a reject_reason, approve forbids both.

nodes.name: DirectiveEditPayload
nodes.summary: Field-level edit map keyed by DirectiveSource ref_id; a validator rejects empty update blocks so an edit verdict always carries substantive changes.

nodes.name: Section02AOperatorVerdict
nodes.summary: Operator verdict record (approve/edit/reject) binding run_id, surface_id, operator, submission time, and the SHA256 decision-card digest; validators enforce UUID4, timezone awareness, digest format, and verb/payload consistency.

nodes.name: canonical_asset_record.v0_1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v0.1 JSON Schema for the canonical asset record — the course-source evidence unit carrying asset kind (reading, lecture, lab, etc.), grounding status (source_grounded through needs_sme_review), source refs with locators, and SME-review flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Emitted from a Pydantic v2 model; uses $defs enums with additionalProperties:false for closed-shape validation.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel package init that re-exports the Marcus dispatch contract surface (DispatchEnvelope, DispatchReceipt, kinds, outcomes, and both builder functions) under the app namespace.

nodes.name: contract.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Marcus-to-specialist dispatch contract: DispatchKind/DispatchOutcome enums, DispatchEnvelope and DispatchReceipt payload models, and builder helpers that stamp correlation IDs and UTC timestamps.

nodes.name: DispatchKind
nodes.summary: Enum of the dispatch categories Marcus can route to specialists (the closed set of specialist work kinds).

nodes.name: DispatchOutcome
nodes.summary: Enum of terminal dispatch result states reported back in a DispatchReceipt.

nodes.name: DispatchEnvelope
nodes.summary: Outbound dispatch payload model carrying run_id, specialist_id, dispatch kind, input packet, context refs, correlation_id, and a UTC timestamp.

nodes.name: DispatchReceipt
nodes.summary: Inbound receipt model a specialist returns after a dispatch — correlation_id, outcome, output artifacts, diagnostics, and duration in milliseconds.

nodes.name: build_dispatch_envelope
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a DispatchEnvelope from dispatch kind, run id, input packet, and context refs, filling in correlation id and timestamp defaults.

nodes.name: build_dispatch_receipt
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a DispatchReceipt tying a specialist's outcome, artifacts, diagnostics, and duration back to the originating correlation id.

nodes.name: dials-spec.md
nodes.summary: Companion spec to the lesson-plan schema documenting the two operator-set dials (enrichment and corroboration), their 0.0-1.0 semantics, null defaults, dial interactions with the source-fitness signal, and canonical operator-facing wording.
nodes.languageNotes: Declares a split authority-of-record: schema.py wins on shape/validators, this doc wins on user-facing language.

nodes.name: html_review_pack.py
nodes.summary: Generates and manages static HTML review packs for gate reviews in a trial run: writes per-gate packs of slide rows with output digests, opens them in a browser with an open-log, archives prior versions, and blocks gate advance until the pack has been opened.

nodes.name: BrowserOpenError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when the review pack cannot be opened in the operator's browser.

nodes.name: GateAdvanceBlockedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a gate advance is attempted before the review pack open-log proves the pack was viewed.

nodes.name: ReviewPackRow
nodes.summary: Row model for one slide entry in a review pack (index, label, preview, output payload) with an output_digest method that sha256-hashes the canonical JSON of the output.

nodes.name: write_review_pack
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered HTML review pack for a trial/gate under the runs root, archiving any existing pack to a versioned history directory first.

nodes.name: open_review_pack
nodes.summary: Opens the review pack in the operator's browser via an injectable opener, raising BrowserOpenError on failure and recording a timestamped open-log used later to permit gate advance.

nodes.name: assert_gate_advance_allowed
nodes.summary: Guard that raises GateAdvanceBlockedError unless the gate's open-log exists and is non-empty, enforcing that the operator viewed the review pack before the gate advances.

nodes.name: _render_review_pack
nodes.summary: Renders the full self-contained HTML document for a gate review pack — header, styles, and one rendered row per slide — with all dynamic text HTML-escaped.

nodes.name: _render_row
nodes.summary: Renders a single slide row of the review pack including escaped preview/output content, verdict radio buttons, and the row's output digest.

nodes.name: learning_event_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Thin wiring layer that records Marcus gate decisions (Gate 2 approval, Gate 3 revision, Gate 4 waiver) as learning events appended to the run's ledger via the learning_event_capture utility.

nodes.name: record_gate_2_decision
nodes.summary: Creates a G2C approval learning event for the run and appends it to the run-directory ledger.

nodes.name: record_gate_3_decision
nodes.summary: Creates a G3 revision learning event for the run and appends it to the run-directory ledger.

nodes.name: record_gate_4_decision
nodes.summary: Creates a G4 waiver learning event for the run and appends it to the run-directory ledger.

nodes.name: stub_dials.py
nodes.summary: Read-only 'coming soon' dials affordance for the plan-drafting conversation surface (Story 30-3a): a frozen Pydantic model pinning Marcus's verbatim line, with no mutator API by contract until the tuning surface lands at 30-3b.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Literal-typed fields plus frozen ConfigDict to make the verbatim Marcus line and read-only mode unforgeable; a contract test asserts the absence of mutator methods.

nodes.name: StubDialsAffordance
nodes.summary: Frozen Pydantic model representing the read-only stub-dials placeholder: literal 'read-only' mode, the pinned Marcus line, and a frozenset of future dial names.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the Marcus pre-dispatch writer package (docstring only).

nodes.name: cp1252_fixture_compare.py
nodes.summary: CLI utility that compares two fixture files byte-wise after newline normalization, reporting equivalence, byte counts, the first divergence offset, and a cp1252-decoded context window as a JSON verdict.
nodes.languageNotes: Decodes divergence context with cp1252 and replacement errors so Windows-encoded fixture bytes render safely in diagnostics.

nodes.name: Cp1252FixtureComparisonVerdict
nodes.summary: Dataclass verdict of a fixture comparison: equivalence flag, byte counts for both sides, first divergence offset, and decoded divergence context.

nodes.name: compare_fixture_bytes
nodes.summary: Reads expected and actual fixture files, normalizes newlines, locates the first byte divergence, and returns a Cp1252FixtureComparisonVerdict with context around the divergence.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: argparse CLI entry point that runs compare_fixture_bytes on two paths and prints the verdict as JSON, returning a non-zero exit code on divergence.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the MCP transport bridge scaffolding package (docstring only).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal package README declaring app.mcp_server as MCP transport bridge scaffolding, justified by FR2, FR16, and FR35.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the MCP server tools subpackage (docstring only).

nodes.name: _frozen_hashes.py
nodes.summary: Exports FROZEN_AT_SHIP_HASHES, a dict of frozen pre-extension sha256 hashes for the g1/g2c/g3/g4 legacy DecisionCard shapes used by migration stories to detect schema drift.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal package README declaring app.models.decision_cards as human-in-the-loop decision card model scaffolding, justified by FR31-FR33 and FR36.

nodes.name: party_mode_contribution.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: v1 JSON Schema for a PartyModeContribution — one persona's contribution to a consolidated party-mode review, with UUID contribution id, persona identity, structured payload, timezone-aware submission timestamp, and optional LangSmith trace link.

nodes.name: glossary_emit.py
nodes.summary: Generates a Markdown glossary from a YAML token registry: loads namespaces and token groups, renders them into dated glossary sections, and writes the output file, with a small CLI main entry.

nodes.name: render_glossary
nodes.summary: Renders the full glossary Markdown text from the parsed registry, iterating namespaces and token groups and stamping the generation date.

nodes.name: emit_glossary
nodes.summary: Loads the YAML token registry, renders the glossary Markdown, creates the output directory, and writes the glossary file.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs emit_glossary with the default registry and output paths.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal package README declaring app.models.state as graph state model scaffolding, justified by FR38, FR46, FR47, and FR50.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Docstring-only package init documenting the NFR-M5 four-file-lockstep convention: every Pydantic state model ships a companion validator file here holding testable cross-field functions its model_validator delegates to (placeholder files for models without cross-field invariants).
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes a repo governance rule ('the validator file exists in the same PR as the model') rather than executable logic.

nodes.name: cache_state_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the CacheState model; ships only a docstring because CacheState has no cross-field invariants.

nodes.name: model_resolution_entry_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the ModelResolutionEntry model; docstring-only because the model's contract is fully field-level.

nodes.name: sanctum_fingerprint_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for SanctumFingerprint, whose invariants (UUID4 identity, tz-aware timestamp, sha256 hex) are all expressible at the field layer, so no module-level functions ship.

nodes.name: specialist_envelope_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the SpecialistEnvelope model; docstring-only since the model has no cross-field invariants.

nodes.name: story_state_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the StoryState model; docstring-only since the model has no cross-field invariants.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Docstring-only package init for the validator companion package of top-level app.models schemas (PipelineRegistry, ModelSelectionPolicy), per the NFR-M5 four-file-lockstep convention.

nodes.name: registry_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder for PipelineRegistry cross-field validators; the default-model-in-entries invariant currently lives inline on the model, with this file reserved as the extraction slot if the logic grows.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marcus-Intake sub-package entry point (steps 01-04 plus 4A pre-packet construction) from the Story 30-1 Marcus duality split; defines the PrePacketSnapshot shape and extract_pre_packet for handing intake state to the orchestrator write API.

nodes.name: PrePacketSnapshot
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass snapshot of intake state (run_id, source_ref, operator_prompt, normalized_payload) handed to the orchestrator write API.

nodes.name: extract_pre_packet
nodes.summary: Builds a PrePacketSnapshot from the intake run state for the pre-packet handoff.

nodes.name: pre_packet.py
nodes.summary: Pre-packet construction and emission for the Marcus-Intake to Irene handoff at step 04/05: builds irene-packet.md from bundle artifacts and, on success, dispatches a pre_packet_snapshot EventEnvelope through an injected orchestrator callable.

nodes.name: prepare_irene_packet
nodes.summary: Pure file-I/O builder that reads bundle artifacts and writes irene-packet.md; byte-identical lift locked at Story 30-2a.

nodes.name: _build_sme_refs
nodes.summary: Assembles SME source references with content digests from bundle metadata.

nodes.name: prepare_and_emit_irene_packet
nodes.summary: Wraps prepare_irene_packet and on success dispatches a pre_packet_snapshot EventEnvelope to a caller-injected orchestrator dispatch callable.

nodes.name: __init__.py
