nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass declaring one comparable artifact: label, description, primary/clone relative paths, and the canonicalizer callables for each side.

nodes.name: ArtifactParityResult
nodes.summary: Per-artifact comparison outcome (presence flags, structural match score, matched/comparable line counts, divergence rationale) with tier1_present and tier2_passes helper properties.

nodes.name: ParityComparisonReport
nodes.summary: Aggregate parity report: roots and baseline paths, comparison timestamp, tier-1/tier-2 scores against thresholds, artifact results, and notes.

nodes.name: _normalize_scalar
nodes.summary: Normalizes a scalar value for comparison: collapses whitespace, strips path prefixes, and replaces run ids, UUIDs, and timestamps with stable placeholders before lowercasing.

nodes.name: _flatten_mapping
nodes.summary: Recursively flattens a nested mapping (dicts and lists) into sorted key-path=value lines with normalized scalars, producing a canonical line set for semantic diffing.

nodes.name: _compare_semantic_lines
nodes.summary: Compares the flattened canonical line sets of primary vs clone mappings, computing a structural match score, matched/comparable counts, and a divergence rationale listing mismatched lines.

nodes.name: compare_actual_substrate_parity
nodes.summary: Walks every ArtifactParitySpec, canonicalizes both sides, scores semantic match per artifact, aggregates tier-1 (presence) and tier-2 (match) scores, and returns a ParityComparisonReport with notes on missing baseline or artifacts.

nodes.name: render_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a ParityComparisonReport as a markdown evidence document with summary tables, per-artifact sections, and pass/fail tier verdicts.

nodes.name: write_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered parity evidence markdown for a report to the given output path.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Argparse CLI entry point: parses bundle/run/baseline/output paths, runs compare_actual_substrate_parity, writes the evidence markdown, and prints a summary.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Brief package readme identifying app.runtime as the Slab 1 runtime substrate package and citing its justifying functional requirements (FR1, FR2, FR3, FR7).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer marking app.runtime as the Slab 1 runtime substrate package scaffold; docstring only.

nodes.name: checkpointer.py
nodes.summary: AsyncPostgresSaver factory for LangGraph checkpoint persistence: resolves DATABASE_URL from the environment, normalizes it via psycopg conninfo, and yields a ready checkpointer as an async context manager with idempotent schema setup.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TYPE_CHECKING-guarded imports and an asynccontextmanager wrapper matching AsyncPostgresSaver.from_conn_string's native API shape.

nodes.name: CheckpointerConfigError
nodes.summary: Named exception raised when checkpointer configuration (DATABASE_URL) is missing or invalid.

nodes.name: resolve_database_url
nodes.summary: Reads DATABASE_URL from the environment, raising CheckpointerConfigError with a named error surface when unset.

nodes.name: resolve_checkpointer_conninfo
nodes.summary: Normalizes the resolved database URL into a psycopg conninfo string via make_conninfo.

nodes.name: make_checkpointer
nodes.summary: Async context manager factory yielding an AsyncPostgresSaver built from the resolved conninfo, running setup() so the checkpoints table schema exists.

nodes.name: cleanup_threads.py
nodes.summary: Operator-invoked CLI that deletes aged LangGraph checkpoint threads from Postgres per the RetentionPolicy (max thread age, retain-completed/failed flags), reading status and timestamp tags from checkpoint metadata; supports dry-run and apply modes and never self-schedules.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents a metadata contract (metadata->>'status', updated_at/created_at) the cleanup SQL consumes; untagged checkpoints are retained by design.

nodes.name: CleanupResult
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass capturing a cleanup run outcome: deleted thread count, dry-run flag, and policy version.

nodes.name: _threads_to_delete
nodes.summary: Filters candidate thread rows against the retention policy's retain_completed and retain_failed flags, returning thread ids eligible for deletion.

nodes.name: cleanup
nodes.summary: Queries the checkpoints table for threads older than the policy's max age, applies retain-flag filtering, and either reports (dry-run) or deletes the eligible threads in a single committed transaction.

nodes.name: _build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse parser with a mutually exclusive --dry-run/--apply mode group and a policy path option.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: loads the retention policy, prints its summary, connects to Postgres via DATABASE_URL, runs cleanup, and returns 0 on success or 1 on named failure surfaces.

nodes.name: retention_policy.py
nodes.summary: Operator-facing checkpoint retention configuration: Pydantic RetentionPolicy model (max thread age, documentation-only cron hint, retain-completed/failed flags) plus a strict YAML loader; deliberately lives under app.runtime rather than graph state.
nodes.languageNotes: cron_hint is validated with a permissive five-field regex on purpose — it is documentation for an externally run cron, not a parsed schedule.

nodes.name: RetentionPolicy
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model for checkpoint retention config (max_thread_age_days, cleanup_cron_hint, retain_completed, retain_failed) with extra='forbid' and a field validator enforcing five-field cron shape.

nodes.name: load_policy
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a RetentionPolicy from a YAML file (default state/config/retention-policy.yaml), failing loudly on missing files or non-mapping content.

nodes.name: retry_policy.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic-aware retry helpers for LangGraph nodes: translates ValidationError from node-boundary model_validate calls into a runtime-owned retryable exception and builds a matching RetryPolicy so transiently invalid provider payloads trigger node retries instead of failing the graph.
nodes.languageNotes: Works around LangGraph's default RetryPolicy not treating raw Pydantic ValidationError as retryable; pairs validate_for_retry with pydantic_retry_policy at node registration.

nodes.name: RetryableValidationNodeError
nodes.summary: Runtime-owned retryable exception wrapping a Pydantic ValidationError with the originating node name so LangGraph retry policies can target it.

nodes.name: validate_for_retry
nodes.summary: Validates a payload against a Pydantic model type, re-raising ValidationError as RetryableValidationNodeError tagged with the node name.

nodes.name: pydantic_retry_policy
nodes.summary: Builds a LangGraph RetryPolicy (interval, backoff, max attempts, jitter) that retries on RetryableValidationNodeError.

nodes.name: override_warning.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema (v1) pinning the ModelOverrideWarning payload shape for operator-visible model-override warnings emitted by the runtime; additive-safe versioned contract.

nodes.name: sanctum_warning.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: JSON Schema (v1) pinning the SanctumWarning payload shape — a single operator-visible sanctum mutation warning — as a versioned runtime contract.

nodes.name: span_tags.py
nodes.summary: LangSmith span-tag contract pin exporting the REQUIRED_SPAN_TAG_KEYS frozenset (trial_id, node_id, agent, lane) that every runtime span must carry; changing the set is a governed downstream-breaking schema change.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Specialist package initializer documenting that Slab 2+ specialist migrations land here, each subpackage carrying a model_config.yaml conforming to SpecialistModelConfig; docstring only.

nodes.name: dispatch_loader.py
nodes.summary: Shared importlib helper that loads and executes a specialist module from an explicit file path, used by specialist dispatch wrappers.

nodes.name: load_module
nodes.summary: Builds an importlib spec from a file path, executes the module, and returns it, raising ImportError when the spec cannot be created.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the scaffold expertise/ contract: where specialists keep plan-phase domain reference material, plus guardrails pinning the canonical 9-node lifecycle chain, ClassVar specialist-ID pinning, and the mandatory generator denylist.

nodes.name: model_config.yaml
nodes.summary: Reference-shape specialist model configuration for the scaffold: gpt-5 default model, a per-node override for the act phase, and temperature 0, sitting at level 2 of the three-level model-override hierarchy.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Near-empty package initializer for shared specialist utilities importable by both Marcus wiring and specialists without violating the specialists-to-orchestrator import ban.

nodes.name: research_intake.py
nodes.summary: Research retrieval intake for Agentic Research Foundations R6: converts wrangled research entries (R4 credibility + R3 triangulation) into provenance items and attribution phrases, guards against fabricated citations, and assembles a durable ResearchIntakePacket for Irene Pass-2 and narration.
nodes.languageNotes: Lives under app.specialists._shared deliberately so both Marcus wiring and Irene can import it — M3 forbids specialists importing app.marcus.orchestrator.

nodes.name: FabricatedCitationError
nodes.summary: Named exception raised when cited source ids do not resolve to any wrangled research entry, closing the fabricate-cite hole.

nodes.name: RetrievalProvenanceItem
nodes.summary: Pydantic model recording one source's retrieval provenance: source id, providers, citation id, evidence hierarchy tier, peer-review flag, triangulation status, reliability score, and attribution phrase.

nodes.name: ResearchIntakePacket
nodes.summary: Pydantic packet summarizing a research intake for a cluster: intake mode, evidence-bolster flag, entries consumed, retrieval provenance list, known losses, and deduplicated attribution phrases.

nodes.name: attribution_phrase_for_entry
nodes.summary: Selects the convergence narration pattern (dual-provider, scite-only, consensus-only, etc.) for a research entry based on its providers and triangulation status.

nodes.name: build_retrieval_provenance
nodes.summary: Maps wrangled research entries into RetrievalProvenanceItem records, skipping malformed entries and attaching computed attribution phrases.

nodes.name: resolvable_source_ids
nodes.summary: Collects the set of citable identifiers (source ids in both original and lowercase form, plus source refs) that legitimately resolve to wrangled entries.

nodes.name: assert_no_fabricated_citations
nodes.summary: Verifies every cited source id resolves against the wrangled entries, raising FabricatedCitationError listing any unresolvable citations.

nodes.name: consume_research_entries
nodes.summary: Top-level intake: builds provenance from entries, records known losses for malformed input, deduplicates attribution phrases, and returns a ResearchIntakePacket for the cluster with the given intake mode and evidence-bolster flag.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Passthrough specialist stub namespace re-exporting PASSTHROUGH_SPECIALIST_ID and passthrough_node so the migrated v4.2 manifest can load, compile, and run end-to-end before any real Slab 2 specialist exists.

nodes.name: passthrough_specialist.py
nodes.summary: No-op LangGraph specialist node that accepts any state and returns an empty dict so Pydantic-state reducers propagate input unchanged; stands in for all 33 v4.2 manifest steps until real 9-node specialist scaffolds replace it, with the _stub prefix keeping it out of real-specialist directory scans.

nodes.name: passthrough_node
nodes.summary: No-op LangGraph node returning an empty dict so the incoming state propagates unchanged.

nodes.name: make_passthrough
nodes.summary: Factory returning the passthrough node callable for a given specialist id, letting the compiler wire named manifest entries to the stub.

nodes.name: README.md

layers.name: Marcus Orchestration
layers.description: The Marcus-SPOC orchestrator, conversational CLI surface, lesson-plan compilation and research projections (research packet, glossary, trends), course-source registry (canonical processed source, SME registry, syllabus metadata), research credibility/detective-gate nodes, and styleguide resolution wiring that drives an instance of the production runtime.

layers.name: Specialist Agents & Composers
layers.description: Per-specialist LangGraph agents (irene, gary, texas, enrique, vision, wanda, kira, motion_planner, quinn_r, and _shared audits including research intake) and the composer nodes — including the Section 02A directive composer — that assemble their outputs into decks, motion, and workbook artifacts.

layers.name: Gates & Workflow Substrate
layers.description: HIL gate decision modules (including the Section 02A G0 gate surface), the pipeline-manifest compiler, Cora block-mode dev-graph enforcement, and ledger/manifest substrate that gate and sequence the production walk.

layers.name: Domain Models
layers.description: Pydantic v2 domain and runtime-state models plus emitted JSON schemas under app/models that define decision-cards, lesson-plan envelopes, contributions side-cars, and the shared data contracts every specialist reads and writes.

layers.name: Runtime & Transport
layers.description: Runtime execution and persistence concerns: Postgres checkpointer/retention, resume-and-recover replay, the LiteLLM Batch transport package, HTTP transport, MCP server, parity checks, and audit plumbing that carry state through the graph.

layers.name: Operator & Developer Tooling
layers.description: Standalone scripts under scripts/ (BaseAPIClient and concrete API clients, content generators, analysis/acoustics diagnostics such as the source-fidelity report, operator utilities) used for live proofing and off-the-books discovery runs.

layers.name: BMAD Agent Skills
layers.description: Operator-facing BMAD agent skill packages under skills/ (SKILL.md persona files, reference contracts, and helper scripts) for the custom production personas Marcus routes work to.

layers.name: Project Root & Configuration
layers.description: Top-level project configuration and metadata: environment templates, git/coverage attributes, and analysis-scope tooling artifacts that sit at the repository root.

tour.title: Runtime Platform Overview
tour.description: Start with the package READMEs for the three pillars of the production runtime: app.models (the shared Pydantic model catalog every component reads and writes), app.manifest (the deterministic pipeline-manifest compiler), and app.runtime (LangGraph persistence — checkpointer, retention, thread cleanup). Together they frame the product goal: Marcus-SPOC, a conversational orchestrator that compiles a manifest into a LangGraph walk and drives specialist agents through human-in-the-loop gates. Keep this triangle — models, manifest, runtime — in mind; every later stop hangs off one of its corners.

tour.title: Marcus-SPOC CLI and Run Lifecycle
tour.description: The operator's front door is `python -m app.marcus.cli`, whose __main__.py builds the argparse tree aggregating trial (start/resume/recover/batch), gate, ad-hoc ask, plan-dialogue, and plan-ratify subcommands. marcus_spoc.py is the Marcus-SPOC conversational surface itself — the single point of contact that owns source material and learning objectives and drives an instance of the production runtime. trial.py wires the lifecycle vocabulary the operator actually types: a 'trial' is one tracked production run of a lesson, with per-run directories, receipts, and economics reporting, and its start / pause-at-gate / resume / recover verbs are the frame for the runner in the next step.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `python -m package` convention executes a package's __main__.py, letting a package double as a CLI without installing console scripts. Argparse subparsers compose independent subcommand trees into one top-level command.

tour.title: Production Runner: Two Walks
tour.description: production_runner.py is the heart of the runtime and one of the highest fan-out files in the graph: it compiles the manifest into a LangGraph and performs TWO node walks — a start walk that intentionally stops at gate G1, and a resume walk that continues after operator verdicts. Any gate-pause side-effect must land in BOTH walks, a subtle invariant that has bitten before. gate_runner.py is the companion that executes individual gate nodes and surfaces their decision cards to the operator.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph models workflows as state graphs whose execution can be interrupted and resumed from a checkpoint. A 'walk' here is one invocation of the compiled graph; pausing at a human-in-the-loop gate means raising an interrupt and persisting state so a later walk can pick up exactly where the first stopped.

tour.title: Manifest Compiler
tour.description: The pipeline is not hand-wired: app/manifest is a tightly coupled cluster where schema.py defines the manifest shape, loader.py reads and validates the YAML pipeline manifest, and compiler.py deterministically compiles it into the node sequence the production runner walks. This manifest-compiled design is why the pipeline is auditable and why manifest edits are governance events, not casual code changes. The runner from Step 3 consumes this compiler's output directly.

tour.title: Gates, Verdicts, and the Ledger
tour.description: Human-in-the-loop gates pause the walk until the operator renders a verdict. resume_api.py (fan-in 28) is the shared surface gate modules use to accept verdicts and resume the graph, and operator_verdict.py is the Pydantic verdict model those surfaces validate against. Every gate event is also persisted to the append-only ledger_events table with idempotency keys — the durable audit trail of who approved what, when.
tour.languageLesson: Append-only event tables with idempotency keys are a classic audit pattern: instead of mutating a status column, every decision is a new immutable row, so replaying or double-submitting a verdict cannot corrupt history.

tour.title: Lesson Plan Model Layer
tour.description: Before any slides or narration exist, the lesson lives as a lesson plan: schema.py (fan-in 18) defines the plan's Pydantic model family, composition.py assembles plan units into a coherent whole, and the frozen lesson_plan.v1.schema.json is the emitted JSON Schema contract that pins the shape. This layer is where SOURCE material and learning objectives — the governing inputs — become typed, gateable plan structures the specialists downstream consume.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 models can emit versioned JSON Schema files that are committed and frozen. Shape-pin tests then diff the emitted schema against the frozen file, turning accidental model changes into loud test failures instead of silent contract drift.

tour.title: Course-Source Registry
tour.description: app/marcus/course_source is the registry that makes source material a first-class, validated citizen. canonical_processed_source.py enforces the ratified lesson-leaf layout, manifest_scan.py deterministically walks git-tracked corpus files and classifies them (with manifest_drift detection against stored snapshots), sme_registry.py resolves per-SME voice/styleguide/attribution, and syllabus_metadata.py extracts course metadata from DOCX/MHTML syllabi. The frozen canonical_asset_record schema is the evidence unit binding it all together — this is how 'SOURCE + LOs are KING' is enforced in code.

tour.title: Section 02A Composer and G0 Gate
tour.description: The Section 02A composer is the first LLM-driven step that touches the corpus from Step 7 — composer.py walks a lesson corpus leaf, applies rule-based exclusions, and classifies each file's role into a typed directive (directive_model.py). The result is not trusted blindly: the Section 02A G0 poll_surface displays the directive with canonical SHA256 decision-card digests and lets the operator view, edit, and ratify it before the run proceeds. This pair is the canonical pattern for 'LLM proposes, operator disposes' at the very start of the walk.

tour.title: State Substrate and Specialist Contract
tour.description: Now the foundation everything above stands on: app/models/state. _base.py (fan-in 55, the most-depended-upon file in the repo) and run_state.py (fan-in 43) define the graph state that flows through every walk, while specialist_envelope.py and specialist_return.py define the typed payload a specialist receives and the typed result it must hand back. contract.py in _scaffold pins the contract every specialist implements — this uniformity is what lets the runner treat twenty different agents identically.

tour.title: Specialist LangGraph Roster
tour.description: Each specialist is its own LangGraph built on a standard 9-node scaffold, differing only in its act body. Irene authors the lesson narrative and collateral, Gary generates Gamma slide decks in classic and studio variants, Texas performs disciplined research retrieval, Tracy enriches research-shaped retrieval intents with a bounded posture selection (the research-posture runtime you will meet in the next step), and Enrique produces directed ElevenLabs voice-over. Because all five honor the envelope/return contract from Step 9, Marcus dispatches to them uniformly — the roster grows by adding graphs, not by changing the runner.

tour.title: Agentic Research Foundations
tour.description: The newest orchestrator capability: at manifest node 04.55, research_wiring.py runs the Irene–Tracy–Texas bridge — it resolves the research posture, dispatches retrieval intents to live Scite/Consensus providers, and mints cited research entries. research_credibility.py classifies every retrieved row onto a closed evidence-hierarchy tier set (T1 systematic review down to unclassified), and research_citation.py mints entries with deterministic source_ref/source_hash and runs the G2 citation-fidelity gate in FAIL mode — a single unsourced claim fails the run. research_detective_gate.py is the R7 hard-pause: Irene Pass-2 cannot dispatch until an operator disposition receipt is on disk. Finally, research_intake.py is the shared R6 surface that converts wrangled entries into provenance items and attribution phrases while guarding against fabricated citations — specialists consume research only through it.
tour.languageLesson: Exception hierarchies can route control flow: the detective gate's error subclasses SpecialistDispatchError, so the production runner's existing _pause_at_error path handles the research hard-pause with zero new runner code — subclassing an established exception enrolls new behavior in old machinery.

tour.title: Workbook and Research Projections
tour.description: Research is only valuable once it lands in a deliverable. research_packet.py is the thin shared reader that loads the node-04.55 research_wiring contribution from run.json into an immutable ResearchPacket, with named consumer resolvers so each downstream consumer witnesses the same rows. Two projections consume it: glossary_projection.py turns tier-labeled rows into encyclopedia glossary articles, and trends_projection.py builds a grounded ResearchTrendsBrief — model-prior-only topics are rejected, every trend claim traces to a retrieved row. Both feed workbook_producer.py, the ModalityProducer that composes the workbook companion to the narrated deck (canonical Markdown plus DOCX from the same content), and the 07W specialist graph is the deterministic 9-node adapter that runs it inside the walk like any other specialist from Step 10.
tour.languageLesson: The projection pattern: one immutable source-of-truth packet feeds multiple read-only 'projections' (glossary, trends), each a pure function from packet rows to a rendered view. Because projections never mutate the packet, adding a new consumer cannot corrupt what other consumers see.

tour.title: Persistence, Economics, and Batch Execution
tour.description: Three runtime concerns make long, gate-paused runs viable and affordable. checkpointer.py resolves Postgres connection settings and builds the LangGraph checkpointer, so a run paused at a gate on Tuesday can resume Thursday with full state intact (Postgres runs natively here — no Docker). economics.py (fan-in high) tracks per-run cost accounting, feeding the trial economics reports the operator sees at run close. On top of both sits the opt-in Batch transport: llm_execution_config.py defines per-node realtime/batch transport profiles, llm_batch_eligibility.py loads the YAML matrix declaring which call sites may batch, and adapter.py drives the provider Files+Batches API with durable per-run BatchReceipts so an interrupted run can rejoin its in-flight batch — trading latency for roughly 50% provider cost.
tour.languageLesson: Provider Batch APIs work over JSONL: each line is a self-contained request tagged with a custom_id, and the async output file is joined back to callers by that id. Because completion can take hours, durable receipts plus a 'waiting' exception type let the run pause and later resume the join instead of blocking.

tour.title: Styleguide Resolution
tour.description: Visual identity is resolved, not hard-coded. resolver.py in app/styleguide is the CD-owned single source of truth for the Gamma styleguide library (re-homed from Gary's module, with a byte-compatible barrel left behind), and parity.py computes sha256-over-canonical-form hashes so any drift between resolution paths is detectable. Upstream, styleguide_picker.py serves a localhost card-grid page at CD-entry, letting the operator pick a style before the run — the choice then flows through resolution into Gary's deck generation from Step 10.
tour.languageLesson: The strangler-fig migration pattern: move a module to its new canonical home, then leave a byte-compatible re-export barrel at the old path so existing importers keep working while callers migrate incrementally.

tour.title: BMAD Agent Skills Layer
tour.description: Finally, the human-facing persona layer that sits beside the production runtime. skills/README.md indexes the custom production skills and BMAD personas, and Marcus's SKILL.md is the activation document an IDE agent reads to embody the orchestrator persona, with capabilities/registry.yaml declaring his typed capability schemas. Remember the guardrail: these persona-driven exploratory runs are a means of discovery — the production codebase you just toured, orchestrated by Marcus-SPOC, is the product.
